mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving real activity and earnings breadth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; negative momentum predicts the opposite.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value for 2007-09-01 was 114.357 as of 2007-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO momentum does not produce a positive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2007-10-01 was 4.7 as of 2007-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or have an average forward return not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts combined with a federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a housing-led demand contraction under restrictive financing conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2007-09-01 was 1191.0 and FEDFUNDS for 2007-10-01 was 4.76
regime_hint: housing downturn with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not generate lower 60-day S&P 500 forward returns than either HOUST weakness or high FEDFUNDS alone in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals policy easing that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 25 basis points over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value for 2007-10-01 was 4.76 as of 2007-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns following the trigger are not higher than the unconditional mean in at least 55% of walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals a policy constraint that prevents rapid easing, raising discount-rate risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2007-10-01 was 208.903 and FEDFUNDS was 4.76
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and high FEDFUNDS has a nonnegative coefficient for 20-day S&P 500 returns in two consecutive expanding-window backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations adjust.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value for 2007-10-01 was 380259.0 as of 2007-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a mean 5-day S&P 500 forward return below the unconditional mean or a hit rate below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth deceleration together with a rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and higher recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates while UNRATE rises over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2007-10-01 was 138421.0 and UNRATE was 4.7
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor rollover trigger produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional negative-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above its recent trend signals broad demand strength and rising aggregate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds its trailing 4-quarter average
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value for 2007-07-01 was 11630.7 as of 2007-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 growth has zero or negative Rank IC with 60-day S&P 500 forward returns across the full walk-forward sample.
